paper: channel x layer surface

magenta/red = mxfp4 (W4A4), green = bf16. Outliers inflate fp4 block scales, so big per-block absmax = more quantization error.
What am I looking at?
SmoothQuant Fig. 4 analog. The famous SmoothQuant plot is a 3D surface of |activation| over (token, channel) showing outliers form ridges along the channel axis -- a few hidden channels are huge for essentially every token. We record per-channel marginals (max over tokens), so here the surface is channel index (x) x decoder layer (y), color = that channel's max |activation| at one representative denoising step. A bright vertical streak = an outlier channel that stays large across depth -- exactly the channel-wise structure SmoothQuant exploits. One row of panels per GEMM site; left = bf16, right = mxfp4. Channels are max-pooled to ~512 columns so spikes survive.

Bright vertical streaks = outlier channels that persist across layers. bf16 (left) vs mxfp4 (right), one denoising step.
